doc_title: van Berg, Kowalski & Wright (2025). Learning-Styles-Matched Instruction: A Meta-Analysis of 124 Studies. Educational Psychology Review, 37(3), 612-651.
label: fabricated

response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 92
REASON: The document fabricates a meta-analysis conclusion that contradicts established scientific consensus—decades of rigorous research (including meta-analyses by Pashler et al. 2008, Chabris et al. 2009) have found no evidence that matching instruction to learning styles improves outcomes, yet this paper claims a substantial pooled effect (d=0.62) across 124 studies, which would be a striking reversal of the existing evidence base that would require major headlines in educational research.
